currentUse: Active Lithuanian Catholic mission of the Archdiocese of Chicago, worshipping inside the community-owned Lithuanian World Center (Pasaulio Lietuvių Centras), 14911 127th St., Lemont, IL 60439
summary: Blessed Jurgis Matulaitis Lithuanian Catholic Mission is alive and fully operating as of 2026 — one of the healthiest entries in the record set. Its official website (copyright 2026, with announcements dated January through April 2026) lists Fr. Artūras Sederevičius, SJ as director, with Sunday Masses at 9:00 a.m., 11:00 a.m. and 6:00 p.m., weekday Masses Tuesday–Saturday at 8:00 a.m., and a bilingual Mass the last Saturday of each month — all otherwise in Lithuanian. The Lithuanian World Center's own organizations page confirms the mission operates inside the Center at 14911 127th St., Lemont, with Birutė O'Brien as office administrator. A full Holy Week and Easter 2026 schedule was published in March 2026, and in August 2024 the mission co-organized a Community Day at the Center drawing roughly 650 Lithuanians and nearly 40 organizations. The mission's Renew My Church exposure took the form of a grouping with Nativity BVM in Chicago's Marquette Park — the archdiocese's only territorial Lithuanian parish — documented in a June 2023 open letter to then-chaplain Algis Baniulis, SJ; both communities continue to operate. There is no sale, demolition, or conversion to report: the venue was never diocesan property, so the mission's real-estate risk profile differs fundamentally from the closed-parish cases on this site.

developments.headline: Full Holy Week and Easter 2026 schedule published — mission fully active
developments.detail: A Lithuanian-American news site published the mission's complete Holy Week and Easter 2026 liturgical schedule (services March 27 – April 12, 2026, including bilingual services with the Celestial Voices choir), confirming an active, fully programmed liturgical life at 14911 127th St., Lemont. The mission's own website carries 2026 announcements including an April 12, 2026 Confirmation and a memorial for Fr. Antanas Saulaitis, SJ.

sources.publisher: Blessed Jurgis Matulaitis Lithuanian Catholic Mission

developments.headline: Mission co-organizes Community Day drawing ~650 Lithuanians to Lemont
developments.detail: The mission co-organized the August 2024 Community Day ("Bendruomenės diena") with the Lithuanian World Center, including an outdoor Mass and an organizations fair with nearly 40 Lithuanian organizations; roughly 650 people attended. Mission representative Birutė Norvaišaitė O'Brien was quoted thanking the community.

sources.title: Į Bendruomenės dieną Lemonte susirinko 650 lietuvių!

developments.headline: Renew My Church grouping with Nativity BVM (Marquette Park) documented
developments.detail: An open letter to then-mission chaplain Algis Baniulis, SJ describes the Archdiocese of Chicago offering Nativity BVM Parish (Chicago's only Lithuanian Catholic parish) and the Blessed Jurgis Matulaitis Mission the opportunity to group together under the Renew My Church program, examining sacramentals, ministry programs, budgets and physical structures. The author urged embracing the grouping as an evangelization opportunity. Both communities continue to operate as of 2026.

sources.title: Renew My Church… An Open Letter to Algis Baniulis, S.J, Mission Chaplain

developments.headline: Current leadership: Fr. Artūras Sederevičius, SJ, continuing the Jesuit chaplaincy
developments.detail: The mission's official website (© 2026) and the Lithuanian World Center's organizations page both list Fr. Artūras Sederevičius, SJ as pastor/director, with Birutė O'Brien as office administrator and office hours Monday–Friday 9:00 a.m.–2:00 p.m. This continues the Jesuit staffing line (Algis Baniulis, SJ was chaplain as of 2023).

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The archive record (standing, diocese_rc canonical function inside the community-owned Pasaulio Lietuvių Centras, survived an RMC grouping) is fully confirmed: the mission operates inside the Lithuanian World Center per lcenter.org and its own site, remains under the Archdiocese of Chicago per GCatholic, and the RMC grouping (with Nativity BVM, Marquette Park) is independently documented in the June 2023 open letter, with both communities still operating in 2026. Minor refinements: (1) the 2023 open letter styles it "Blessed Jurgis Matulaitis, MIC Mission" (Marian Fathers designation) even though the chaplaincy has been Jesuit-staffed (Baniulis SJ in 2023, Sederevičius SJ now) — a naming quirk, not a conflict. (2) One search-result summary gave a founding year of 1988 vs. the archive's 1990, but I could not verify 1988 on any page I actually opened, so the archive's 1990 stands unchallenged.
gaps: Founding-year detail (1988 vs 1990) could not be settled from opened pages — neither the official site excerpt, lcenter.org, nor GCatholic states a founding date. The formal outcome document of the Nativity BVM/Matulaitis RMC grouping (archdiocesan decision announcement) was not located; the grouping is evidenced only by the 2023 open letter, and continuation of both communities is inferred from their active 2026 web presences. The exact date Fr. Sederevičius succeeded prior chaplains (Baniulis SJ, and a Draugas interview references Fr. Vaidas Lukoševičius at the mission, page not opened) was not established. No property-transaction research applies: the venue is the community-owned Lithuanian World Center, not diocesan real estate, so no sale/buyer/price questions arise.
